competed at the **[2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2018_Commonwealth_Games "2018 Commonwealth Games")** in [Gold Coast](/wiki/Gold_Coast,_Queensland "Gold Coast, Queensland"), Australia between 4 and 15 April 2018.[[1]](#cite_note-1) It was England's 21st appearance at the Commonwealth Games, having participated at every Games since their inception in [1930](/wiki/1930_British_Empire_Games "1930 British Empire Games").

**[England](/wiki/England "England")** competed at the **[2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2018_Commonwealth_Games "2018 Commonwealth Games")** in [Gold Coast](/wiki/Gold_Coast,_Queensland "Gold Coast, Queensland"), Australia between 4 and 15 April 2018.[[1]](#cite_note-1) It was England's 21st appearance at the Commonwealth Games, having participated at every Games since their inception in [1930](/wiki/1930_British_Empire_Games "1930 British Empire Games").

England sent a total of 394 athletes across all 18 sports, making it the largest team ever to represent the nation at an overseas sporting event.[[2]](#cite_note-2) However, only 388 athletes competed. Triathlete [Alistair Brownlee](/wiki/Alistair_Brownlee "Alistair Brownlee") was the country's flag bearer during the [opening ceremony](/wiki/2018_Commonwealth_Games_opening_ceremony "2018 Commonwealth Games opening ceremony").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

With [Birmingham](/wiki/Birmingham "Birmingham") as the host of the [2022 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2022_Commonwealth_Games "2022 Commonwealth Games"), the English segment was performed during the Closing Ceremony.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Athletics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=England_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Athletics")]

Main article: [Athletics at the 2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "Athletics at the 2018 Commonwealth Games")

On 24 October 2017, [Commonwealth Games England](/wiki/Commonwealth_Games_England "Commonwealth Games England") announced a squad of 75 athletes consisting of 35 men and 40 women.[[4]](#cite_note-7) Since the initial announcement, selected relay athletes were given permission to fill any remaining slots in the individual sprints.[[5]](#cite_note-8)

On 11 December 2017, Commonwealth Games England announced the selection of eighteen athletes to compete in the para-athletic events in Gold Coast.[[6]](#cite_note-9) [Jade Jones](/wiki/Jade_Jones_(athlete) "Jade Jones (athlete)") will compete in both the para-athletics and paratriathlon.

**Key**:

* **Q** = Qualified for the next round
* **q** = Qualified for the next round as a fastest loser *or*, in field events, by position without achieving the qualifying target
* DNS = Did not start
* DNF = Did not finish
* DSQ = Disqualified
* **GR** = Games record
* **NR** = National record
* N/A = Round not applicable for the event
* Bye = Athlete not required to compete in round

**Note**–Ranks given for track events are across all heats

## Basketball

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=England_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Basketball")]

Main article: [Basketball at the 2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Basketball_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "Basketball at the 2018 Commonwealth Games")

England has qualified both [men's](/wiki/England_men%27s_national_basketball_team "England men's national basketball team") and [women's](/wiki/England_women%27s_national_basketball_team "England women's national basketball team") teams for a total of 24 athletes. The men's team was invited by [FIBA](/wiki/FIBA "FIBA") and the [CGF](/wiki/Commonwealth_Games_Federation "Commonwealth Games Federation"), while the women's team qualified as being one of the top three ranked teams in the Commonwealth (minus the host nation Australia).[[8]](#cite_note-11) Each team will consist of twelve players.

Both teams were announced on 16 March 2018.[[9]](#cite_note-12)

## Gymnastics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=England_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Gymnastics")]

Main article: [Gymnastics at the 2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Gymnastics_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "Gymnastics at the 2018 Commonwealth Games")

On 12 February 2018, Team England announced its squad of 3 rhythmic gymnasts.[[15]](#cite_note-18) The initial 10-strong artistic gymnastics squad (5 men and 5 women) was added on 21 February 2018[[16]](#cite_note-19) and the current make-up is correct as of 28 March 2018.

## Hockey

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=England_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Hockey")]

Main article: [Hockey at the 2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Hockey_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "Hockey at the 2018 Commonwealth Games")

England qualified both a men's and women's hockey team by placing in the top nine (excluding the host nation, Australia) among Commonwealth nations in the [FIH World Rankings](/wiki/FIH_World_Rankings "FIH World Rankings") as of 31 October 2017. Each team consists of 18 athletes, for a total of 36.[[17]](#cite_note-qualification-20)

The men's team was announced on 12 March 2018.[[18]](#cite_note-21) Following an initial announcement on 15 March 2018,[[19]](#cite_note-22) the women's team was confirmed on 20 March 2018.[[20]](#cite_note-23)

## Lawn bowls

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=England_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Lawn bowls")]

Main article: [Lawn bowls at the 2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "Lawn bowls at the 2018 Commonwealth Games")

On 13 November 2017, [Commonwealth Games England](/wiki/Commonwealth_Games_England "Commonwealth Games England") announced a full squad consisting of 15 bowlers, 9 of which are male and 6 female. Along with the other athletes, 2 directors were announced, Mark and Sue Wherry, who will assist the visually impaired athletes in the para-lawn bowls.[[21]](#cite_note-24)

## Netball

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=England_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Netball")]

Main article: [Netball at the 2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Netball_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "Netball at the 2018 Commonwealth Games")

England qualified a netball team by virtue of being ranked in the top 11 (excluding the host nation, Australia) of the [INF World Rankings](/wiki/INF_World_Rankings "INF World Rankings") on 1 July 2017.[[22]](#cite_note-25)[[23]](#cite_note-26)[[24]](#cite_note-27)

The squad was announced on 14 February 2018.[[25]](#cite_note-28)

## Rugby sevens

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=England_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Rugby sevens")]

Main article: [Rugby sevens at the 2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Rugby_sevens_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "Rugby sevens at the 2018 Commonwealth Games")

England qualified both [men's](/wiki/England_national_rugby_sevens_team "England national rugby sevens team") and [women's](/wiki/England_women%27s_national_rugby_sevens_team "England women's national rugby sevens team") teams by finishing as one of the top nine Commonwealth Games Association sides in the [2016-17 World Rugby Sevens Series](/wiki/2016-17_World_Rugby_Sevens_Series "2016-17 World Rugby Sevens Series") and as one of the top six sides at the [2016-17 World Rugby Women's Sevens Series](/wiki/2016-17_World_Rugby_Women%27s_Sevens_Series "2016-17 World Rugby Women's Sevens Series") respectively, as of 1 July 2017.[[17]](#cite_note-qualification-20) Each team gets 12 players and a reserve.

The women's squad was announced on 26 March 2018.[[28]](#cite_note-31) The men's squad was provisionally selected a few days later, although it was not officially announced until 3 April 2018.[[29]](#cite_note-32)

## Swimming

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=England_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games&action=edit&section=30 "Edit section: Swimming")]

Main article: [Swimming at the 2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Swimming_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "Swimming at the 2018 Commonwealth Games")

Team England announced its squad in two tranches.

The first tranche of 15 swimmers was announced on 4 October 2017.[[32]](#cite_note-35) After the *Swim England National Winter Championships*, a second tranche of 24 swimmers was announced on 5 January 2018.[[33]](#cite_note-36)

[Jonathan Fox](/wiki/Jonathan_Fox_(swimmer) "Jonathan Fox (swimmer)") and [Oliver Hynd](/wiki/Oliver_Hynd "Oliver Hynd") have since withdrawn after their recent reclassifications by the [IPC](/wiki/International_Paralympic_Committee "International Paralympic Committee").[[34]](#cite_note-37)
